ja-gram-n3-705-fill-0: 〜な is a prohibition command meaning 'don't do.' In this sentence it forbids doing something stupid. Unlike なさい (positive command), な directly blocks the action.
ja-gram-n3-705-fill-1: 〜な prohibits an action ('don't drink'). Here it warns against excess, not recommending an action like 方がいい would.
ja-gram-n3-705-fill-2: 〜な blocks foolish behavior ('don't be silly'). This negative command differs from 方が which suggests a better option instead.
ja-gram-n3-705-fill-3: 〜な orders people not to move in this urgent context. It's a prohibition, not advice (べき) or permission request (てもいいですか).
ja-gram-n3-705-fill-4: 〜な forbids worrying ('don't worry'). This flat prohibition differs from なさい which actively commands you to do something.
ja-gram-n3-786-fill-0: 方が expresses preference when comparing two options. March 1 is more convenient, making it the better choice versus other dates.
ja-gram-n3-786-fill-1: 方が asks if calling after 9:00 is cheaper—comparing costs to find the better deal. This comparison differs from べき (should) or な (don't).
ja-gram-n3-786-fill-2: 方が in negative context (wouldn't know = wouldn't prefer). It signals comparison of better/worse states, not command (な) or obligation (べき).
ja-gram-n3-786-fill-3: 方が means slow but sure is better than fast but flaky—comparing two strategies. The comparison structure differs from a direct command.
ja-gram-n3-786-fill-4: 方が expresses preference: 'fish is better than meat.' It compares merit, unlike なさい (command to do) or ものだから (excuse).
ja-gram-n3-658-fill-0: なさい is an informal imperative ordering people to sit. The context (directed instruction) shows active command, not permission (てもいいですか) or suggestion (方が).
ja-gram-n3-658-fill-1: なさい urgently commands 'do it quickly!' It's a forceful action order, unlike な (prohibition) or べき (obligation without force).
ja-gram-n3-658-fill-2: なさい commands 'shape up!' in frustration—active ordering, not suggesting better behavior (方が) or forbidding (な).
ja-gram-n3-658-fill-3: なさい orders 'eat your vegetables'—direct parental command. Unlike 方が (this would be better), なさい demands action immediately.
ja-gram-n3-658-fill-4: なさい commands 'sit down' as a directive. It's imperative force, contrasting with な (don't) and 方が (this would be better).
ja-gram-n3-1723-fill-0: 〜てもいいですか asks permission politely ('may I borrow?'). It's a request for consent, unlike な (prohibition) or なさい (command).
ja-gram-n3-1723-fill-1: 〜てもいいですか politely requests 'may I go home?' It seeks allowance, contrasting with な (forbidden) and べき (obligation).
ja-gram-n3-1723-fill-2: 〜てもいいですか asks permission about eating apples. The polite question form seeks approval, not command (なさい) or prohibition (な).
ja-gram-n3-1723-fill-3: 〜てもいいですか politely asks 'do you mind if I sit here?' It's permission-seeking, unlike べき (should do) or な (don't).
ja-gram-n3-1723-fill-4: 〜てもいいですか asks 'can I talk to my teacher?' It's seeking allowance, not commanding (なさい) or suggesting better action (方が).
ja-gram-n3-1238-fill-0: がち describes a tendency to get angry easily, especially when negative. Unlike っぽい (just seems ish), がち emphasizes actual pattern behavior.
ja-gram-n3-1238-fill-1: がち shows misunderstandings tend to occur—ongoing pattern. Unlike やすい (easy to do once), がち means it frequently happens.
ja-gram-n3-1238-fill-2: がち marks mainly cloudy forecast—weather tendency. Unlike いっぽい (looks cloudy) or やすい (easy to become), がち is predictable pattern.
ja-gram-n3-1238-fill-3: がち describes habitually reserved/timid nature. It's characteristic tendency, not temporary feeling (っぽい) or ease of action (やすい).
ja-gram-n3-1238-fill-4: がち shows rich people tend to look down on poor—learned behavioral pattern. Unlike つい (accidentally do), がち is intentional tendency.
ja-gram-n3-1530-fill-0: っぽい makes John seem easily angered—gives characteristic impression. Unlike がち (actual tendency pattern), っぽい is perception/feel.
ja-gram-n3-1530-fill-1: っぽい gives expression a childish ring—impression/quality. Unlike にくい (hard to understand), っぽい describes how it feels, not difficulty.
ja-gram-n3-1530-fill-2: っぽい makes appearance cute-ish—adding characteristic feel/quality. It's more impressionistic than がち (actual pattern) or にくい (difficulty).
ja-gram-n3-1530-fill-3: っぽい conveys -ish quality in 'what does that mean -ish!' Unlike がち (actual tendency), っぽい expresses vague/characteristic feel.
ja-gram-n3-1530-fill-4: っぽい calls Laura sexy with characteristic allure—giving appealing impression. Unlike がち (learned behavior), っぽい is perceptual quality.
ja-gram-n3-1408-fill-0: にくい makes him hard to understand—expressing difficulty in comprehending. Unlike やすい (easy to understand), にくい is the opposite difficulty.
ja-gram-n3-1408-fill-1: にくい describes foods hard to eat physically. Unlike やすい (easy to eat), にくい means consuming them poses difficulty.
ja-gram-n3-1408-fill-2: にくい means the rare stamp is hard to acquire. Unlike やすい (easy to find), にくい shows scarcity makes obtaining it difficult.
ja-gram-n3-1408-fill-3: にくい says explanation is hard to understand. Unlike やすい (easy to grasp), にくい means clarity is difficult.
ja-gram-n3-1408-fill-4: にくい makes reading English books difficult. Unlike やすい (easy to read), にくい shows language barrier creates comprehension problems.
ja-gram-n3-1645-fill-0: やすい shows playing inside is easy on rainy days. Unlike にくい (hard to do), やすい emphasizes ease/naturalness of the action.
ja-gram-n3-1645-fill-1: やすい says Japanese becomes easy to understand with daily practice. Unlike がち (tends to happen), やすい means facility/ease improves.
ja-gram-n3-1645-fill-2: やすい warns wet stairs are slippery (easy to slip). Unlike にくい (hard to slip), やすい shows danger/likelihood of the action.
ja-gram-n3-1645-fill-3: やすい makes these shoes easier to put on than those. Unlike がち (tendency), やすい emphasizes comparative ease of action.
ja-gram-n3-502-fill-0: からして means 'even hiragana'—starting from basics, can't even read. Unlike みたい (seems like), からして judges from observable facts.
ja-gram-n3-502-fill-1: からして judges country lacks food, implying worse shortages ahead. Unlike だろう (probably), からして reasons from what you see now.
ja-gram-n3-502-fill-2: からして judges from his poor health that sports is unwise. Unlike とする (assume), からして observes actual condition to conclude.
ja-gram-n3-502-fill-3: からして even his attitude is nasty—judging from behaviors. Unlike として (trying to), からして assesses what's visible.
ja-gram-n3-502-fill-4: からして given the price, expects X machine unlike Y machine. Unlike みたい (seems), からして reasons from concrete fact (price).
ja-gram-n3-541-fill-0: とす assumes we go to Japan as hypothesis. Unlike として (trying to), とす sets condition for reasoning/calculation.
ja-gram-n3-541-fill-1: とする assumes temperature stays constant in physics. Unlike だろう (probably), とする sets a working assumption for logic.
ja-gram-n3-541-fill-2: とする means 'if you aim to be good citizen'—setting volitional assumption. Unlike として (trying to), とする frames as logical condition.
ja-gram-n3-541-fill-3: とする describes trying hard to succeed in life. This use emphasizes volition/effort, unlike だろう (probably will).
ja-gram-n3-541-fill-4: とする in 'be willing to learn' sets condition. Unlike として (attempting), とする frames as mental posture/commitment.
ja-gram-n3-1597-fill-0: として means trying to do two things at once, when attempted simultaneously. Unlike とする (assuming), として shows action-in-progress attempting.
ja-gram-n3-1597-fill-1: として shows children try to prove independence—demonstrating/attempting. Unlike だろう (probably), として describes their active effort.
ja-gram-n3-1597-fill-2: として in trying to eat ice cream, discovered no spoon—when attempting. Unlike みたい (seems), として shows actual attempt before problem.
ja-gram-n3-1486-fill-0: それで links cause to result: overslept, so was late. Unlike ものだから (excuse), それで simply connects action to consequence.
ja-gram-n3-1486-fill-1: それで proposes plan: 'let's catch 3 o'clock bus, okay?' It's sequential decision, not reason (ことから) or excuse (ものだから).
ja-gram-n3-1486-fill-2: それで asks 'then what happened?'—continuation. Unlike ことから (because), それで is narrative sequence forward.
ja-gram-n3-1486-fill-3: それで means next step is just filling card. Unlike せいで (blaming), それで is procedural continuation.
ja-gram-n3-1486-fill-4: それで absolves from further duty—result of action. Unlike ものだから (justifying), それで states simple consequence.
ja-gram-n3-1320-fill-0: ことから reasons observable fact → conclusion: long queues mean popular shop. Unlike おかげで (thanks to), ことから is pure logical inference.
ja-gram-n3-1320-fill-1: ことから reasons from facts: old streets + temples = called Little Kyoto. Unlike おかげで (positive thanks), ことから just observes facts→conclusion.
ja-gram-n3-1320-fill-2: ことから means starting from text design, handle all signboard needs. Unlike それで (sequential), ことから ranges/extends from this base.
ja-gram-n3-1320-fill-3: ことから notes discussion is about Oshamu, so off-topic now. Unlike せいで (blame), ことから states observable fact reason.
ja-gram-n3-1320-fill-4: から (short form) in 'that's how it should be'—simple because. Unlike ものだから (excuse), から is basic causal connection.
ja-gram-n3-55-fill-4: おかげで credits positive outcome to person: thanks to you I learned lesson. Unlike せいで (blaming), おかげで appreciates positive cause.
ja-gram-n3-1499-fill-0: し stacks shared experience: pioneers were hard, children too. Unlike て (sequence), し lists multiple parallel reasons/facts.

ja-gram-n3-1499-fill-2: し stacks life experiences: rock climb, deep dive, sleep in jungle. Unlike おかげで (thanks to), し just enumerates actions done.
ja-gram-n3-1499-fill-3: し gives promotion reason for happiness, and marriage too. Unlike なぜなら (because/why), し just accumulates reasons.
ja-gram-n3-1499-fill-4: し: mother happy, and so was I—parallel emotions. Unlike おかげで (thanks to), し just connects equivalent states.
ja-gram-n3-1163-fill-0: から (with ものです) means house is narrow, so can't fit big furniture. Unlike ことから (observable fact), ものだから is unavoidable fact→result.
ja-gram-n3-1163-fill-1: ものだから: only child, so became selfish—inherent consequence. Unlike ことから (just observation), ものだから shows natural outcome.

ja-gram-n3-1163-fill-3: から (with ものです) = feel hot, so jacket off. Unlike おかげで (thanks), ものだから is unavoidable physical fact.

ja-gram-n3-1788-fill-0: ことで means listening to Japanese music helps improve. The means/method sense differs from おかげで (thanks to fortunate cause).
ja-gram-n3-1788-fill-1: ことで by walking out, you do the right thing—action produces result. Unlike ものだから (excuse), ことで is enabling method.
ja-gram-n3-1788-fill-2: ことで by daily practice, Japanese becomes fluent—method→skill. Unlike せいで (blame), ことで is positive facilitating means.
ja-gram-n3-1583-fill-0: せいで blames lack of sleep for today's haziness. Unlike おかげで (positive thanks), せいで emphasizes negative responsibility.
ja-gram-n3-1583-fill-1: せいで blames him for scolding: his fault I was blamed. Unlike おかげで (gratitude), せいで assigns negative responsibility.

ja-gram-n3-1778-fill-0: からして: because balance sheet is red, aim for black next year—judging from current situation. Unlike ものだから (excuse), からして observes fact.
ja-gram-n3-1778-fill-1: からして: such rumors circulate, so marriage coming soon—reasoning from evidence. Unlike おかげで (positive), からして judges from observable condition.
ja-gram-n3-1300-fill-0: に比べて compares this year to last year's rain. Unlike より (simple comparison), に比べて emphasizes contrast between two periods.
ja-gram-n3-1300-fill-1: に比べて contrasts no-puncture tires against air tires—longer life. Unlike くらい (approximate), に比べて is explicit comparison.
ja-gram-n3-1300-fill-2: に比べて shows this work improves on your last one. Unlike より (than), に比べて stresses measured progress.
ja-gram-n3-1300-fill-3: に比べて contrasts this book against that—both useful, this more interesting. Unlike ほど (extent), に比べて is direct contrast.
ja-gram-n3-1300-fill-4: に比べて marks great progress from before. Unlike より (simple than), に比べて emphasizes scale of improvement.

ja-gram-n3-1508-fill-0: 比べものにならない: planes vs ships on speed—too different to compare. Unlike より (ranks them), this says comparison fails.
